2605.18511.title: A Practical Noise2Noise Denoising Pipeline for High-Throughput Raman Spectroscopy
2605.18511.paper_summary: A lightweight and reproducible denoising pipeline for high-throughput Raman spectroscopy is presented. The approach relies on a one-dimensional convolutional autoencoder trained using a Noise2Noise strategy, requiring neither external spectral libraries nor high signal-to-noise reference spectra for training. From a reduced training subset composed of repeated short-exposure acquisitions, the model learns to reconstruct Raman spectra while efficiently suppressing stochastic noise. The method is evaluated on a heterogeneous mineral sample, using both quantitative spectral fidelity metrics (RMSE, SNR, SSIM) and task-oriented criteria based on unsupervised K-means classification. Results demonstrate that integration times as short as 5 ms per spectrum, which are typically insufficient for reliable interpretation, yield denoised spectra with high fidelity to the reference data while preserving chemically coherent maps. This work provides a practical trade-off between spectral quality and acquisition speed, enabling fast, adaptable Raman workflows compatible with routine laboratory use. It also offers a transferable framework for other one-dimensional spectroscopic modalities.
2605.18511.paper_summary_zh: 一個輕量且可重現的去噪流程針對高通量拉曼光譜進行了介紹。該方法依賴於使用Noise2Noise策略訓練的一維卷積自編碼器，訓練過程中不需要外部光譜庫或高信噪比的參考光譜。模型從由重複短曝光獲取組成的減少訓練子集學習重建拉曼光譜，同時有效抑制隨機噪聲。該方法在一個異質礦物樣本上進行評估，使用定量光譜保真度指標（RMSE、SNR、SSIM）和基於無監督K-means分類的任務導向標準。結果顯示，對於每個光譜的集成時間短至5毫秒，這通常不足以進行可靠的解釋，但仍能產生與參考數據高度一致的去噪光譜，同時保留化學一致的圖譜。這項工作在光譜質量和獲取速度之間提供了實用的權衡，使快速、可調整的拉曼工作流程能夠與常規實驗室使用相容。它還提供了一個可轉移的框架，適用於其他一維光譜學模式。
2605.18511.author: David Martin-Calle et.al.
2605.18511.authors: David Martin-Calle, Cesar Alvarez Llamas, Vincent Motto- Ros, Christophe Dujardin, Jérémie Margueritat, David Rodney
